14.reason: The size of the ans array (50) is too small. With up to 20 terms in each polynomial, the product can have up to 400 terms before merging. This leads to buffer overflow. It should be at least 400 or larger.

44.reason: [This merging logic only merges consecutive duplicate exponents once and only forward. It fails to handle cases where more than two terms have the same exponent or when duplicates are not adjacent after sorting. Also, it overwrites ans[i+1] but doesn't remove it, leading to leftover zero-coefficient terms that might interfere if not filtered properly. More critically, it doesn't accumulate all like terms—only pairwise from left to right.]

More critically, it doesn't accumulate all like terms—only pairwise from left to right.]
